rationale: Bucklin and Sismeiro is cited not only in a grouped literature review but also in the model-development section to justify a core behavioral mechanism: repeated website use creates learning or cognitive lock-in that can reduce revisit costs. That mechanism is directly embedded in how the citing paper specifies and interprets carryover effects in the visit-stage utility and cost structure. Although the citing paper does not adopt the cited paper's exact econometric model, the citation materially informs its conceptual setup and positioning, so it is more than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Bucklin and Sismeiro (2003) as prior evidence on within-site learning and lock-in effects to motivate its carryover-cost mechanism in the visit stage. It then builds a broader multichannel attribution framework that goes beyond within-site browsing behavior.
